initial_request_score.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: macromolecular structure such as protein-DNA/RNA complexes
from a single cryo-EM map
extend the functionality of DeepTracer-1.0 by incorporating the identification of nucleic acids along with amino acids
percent matching averages of Cα at 85-90% and lower root-mean-square deviation (RMSD) values

issues.reason: Text explicitly attributes 85-90% matching to DeepTracer-1.0 (reference [5]) in the Introduction background, though Table 1 shows DeepTracer-2.0 achieved 85.32%. It is a maintained baseline rather than a new specific goal of 2.0.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Clarify that 85-90% matching is the baseline performance from DeepTracer-1.0 that DeepTracer-2.0 aims to maintain while adding nucleic acid modeling.

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: The segmentation network architecture separates density maps... in the DeepTracer-2.0 pipeline
Haruspex utilizes CNN... input that contains the input of 40 [3] voxels segments

issues.reason: Text explicitly states 40³ voxels for Haruspex. While DeepTracer-2.0 uses the segmentation architecture, the 40³ parameter is not explicitly re-stated for DeepTracer-2.0 specifically, though implied.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Specify that the 40³ voxel segmentation is based on the Haruspex architecture utilized within the DeepTracer-2.0 pipeline.

evidence.quote: normalize the density value of each voxel from a range of 0 to 1 and resize each map into a 64 [3] voxel size
initial amino acid network was trained with 1800 experimental maps
293 EMDB/PDB pairs were selected... resolution was 4 Å or better... at least 20 base pairs or larger
four channel outputs... phosphate atom (P), a carbon one atom (C1'), a carbon four atom (C4') or no atom
backbone U-Net determines if each voxel is part of the sugar phosphate backbone, part of the nitrogenous base or not in either group. This has three different channel outputs
The Brickworx model then uses the P atoms and Cryo-EM map to finish modeling the nucleotide
phosphate atoms that are within 8 Å from each other

step_scores.label: partially_supported

evidence.quote: separate postprocessing steps to add on atoms and complete

issues.claim: Cα position within 3 Å of model
issues.reason: Text defines 3 Å threshold as an evaluation metric for matching residues (p4/p5), not explicitly as a postprocessing connection parameter.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Remove the specific 3 Å threshold from postprocessing parameters or clarify it is an evaluation metric, not a processing threshold.

evidence.quote: protein and DNA/RNA structures can be combined to give a final prediction
20 selected cryoEM maps... resolution of 2-4 Å... nucleotide chains are at least 10 nucleic acids or larger
Phenix Suite, using the density maps... version 1.19
metric comparisons are made between Phenix's pipeline performance and DeepTracer's pipeline performance
DeepTracer... 0.638 [RMSD]... 85.32 [% matching]... Phenix... 1.017... 65.44
DeepTracer's pipeline was exponentially faster when compared with Phenix's pipeline
